title: House Appropriations Committee approves $55.5 billion for U.S. Space Force
summary: The U.S. House Appropriations Committee has approved $55.5 billion in funding for the U.S. Space Force. The committee also emphasized the need for greater competition in satellite communications and criticized funding methods like reconciliation for the Golden Dome initiative.

title: Canceled NASA exploration projects suffered billions of dollars in overruns
summary: Four NASA exploration projects were canceled due to significant cost overruns, with expenses more than doubling and over $1 billion in additional increases expected.

title: China schedules Long March 10B rocket launch and recovery attempt
summary: China is planning the debut flight of its Long March 10B rocket in July, aiming to attempt a first-stage recovery at sea. The mission involves airspace and maritime safety measures for the launch.

title: Space Force seeks fresh bidders for satellite-control antennas
summary: The U.S. Space Force is seeking new bidders for satellite-control antennas after terminating a $1.7 billion contract. The program, known as SCAR, will be relaunched with fresh procurement.

title: Shield Space and ClearSpace partner to defend satellites from orbital threats
summary: Shield Space and ClearSpace have partnered to develop solutions for defending satellites against orbital threats using Shield Space's autonomous satellite operations software and ClearSpace's in-orbit servicing capabilities.

title: MDA Space wins contract for Radarsat replenishment satellite
summary: MDA Space has secured a contract to build a radar imaging satellite for the Canadian Space Agency. The satellite will serve as a replenishment for the existing Radarsat system while the government explores next-generation options.

title: Vast signs additional partners for commercial space station microgravity research
summary: Vast has expanded its network of partners for microgravity research and manufacturing on its commercial space station. The company announced the addition of several new collaborators on June 24.

title: York satellite demonstrates two-way UHF communications from low Earth orbit
summary: York Space Systems announced that a satellite built for the U.S. Space Force successfully demonstrated two-way tactical communications using UHF links from low Earth orbit. This achievement marks a significant milestone in tactical satellite communications.

title: How space weather could bust the AI boom
summary: Space weather poses a significant threat to the expansion of AI data centers due to the strain it could place on America's electric grid. The increasing demand from power-hungry AI facilities exacerbates the grid's vulnerability to disruptions caused by solar activity.

title: Vantor selects BAE Systems to build next-generation imaging satellites
summary: Vantor has selected BAE Systems to develop its next-generation high-resolution imaging satellites. This partnership reunites Vantor with the former Ball Aerospace team, which previously contributed to DigitalGlobe's satellite programs.

title: Summer solstice seen from space | Space photo of the day for June 25, 2026
summary: A European weather satellite captured a striking image of the summer solstice from space. The photo was featured as the Space Photo of the Day for June 25, 2026.

title: New to drone flying? Our expert thinks this five-star drone gives you 'everything you could possibly need' and it's $133 off
summary: A five-star review highlights the DJI Mini 4K drone as an excellent choice for beginners, now available at a 35% discount during Prime Day. The drone features two batteries for extended flight time and strong wind resistance.

title: Another 'Star' is born: SpaceX names AI megaconstellation 'Starmind'
summary: SpaceX has named its upcoming AI megaconstellation 'Starmind,' following its tradition of stellar-themed names. This initiative marks a significant step in the company's expansion into AI-driven space technologies.

title: Watch "Avatar: Fire and Ash" with Disney+ and Hulu — get up to 50% off this streaming bundle
summary: Disney+ and Hulu are offering a 50% discount on their streaming bundle, including access to 'Avatar: Fire and Ash' and other franchises like Star Wars and Marvel. The promotion saves subscribers $11.99 per month.

title: Traveling to see the total solar eclipse this August? This solar binocular Prime Day deal is a total-ly bright idea!
summary: A solar binocular deal from Celestron is being promoted for safely viewing the total solar eclipse in August. The EclipSmart 12x50 binoculars are suitable for eclipse viewing and other uses.

title: What time is it on the moon? The US and China disagree
summary: The US and China are in disagreement over the establishment of a lunar standard time, highlighting the need for a unified timekeeping system on the Moon. This issue is crucial for future lunar missions and international cooperation.

title: We praised this telescope's 'legendary performance,' and the whole range is even cheaper for Prime Day
summary: Celestron NexStar SE go-to telescopes are praised for their performance and are discounted for Prime Day. The entire range is now more affordable.

title: I regret missing Project Hail Mary on the big screen but I'm not skipping this cheapest-ever UK Prime Day Lego deal
summary: A Lego set inspired by the starship from Project Hail Mary is available at a 16% discount on Amazon UK, the lowest price ever recorded.

title: My nephew still uses this kids' star projector every night — get it for just $15 at Amazon on Prime Day
summary: A kids' star projector is being offered at a discounted price of $15 on Amazon Prime Day. The durable, kid-approved product features a 90-minute auto-timer for peaceful sleep.

title: A Turquoise Tint for the Black Sea
summary: A turquoise tint appeared in the Black Sea and nearby waters in spring and summer 2026 due to phytoplankton blooms. This phenomenon added a milky blue hue to the waters.

title: NASA’s TESS Mission Reveals the “Puffiest” Planets Ever Found
summary: NASA's TESS mission discovered two new 'super-puff' planets with densities comparable to cotton candy. These Jupiter-sized worlds are the puffiest planets ever identified.

title: NASA at the Ion: Orion Lessons from Artemis II Shape NASA’s Moon to Mars Path
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft returned four astronauts from Artemis II, the first crewed mission around the Moon since Apollo. The mission's lessons are shaping NASA's plans for lunar return and future Mars missions.

title: La NASA compartirá los últimos avances del programa Base Lunar
summary: NASA will share updates on its Lunar Base program, including a virtual discussion led by Administrator Jared Isaacman on June 30. The agency is advancing plans to build a lunar surface base.

title: NASA to Share Latest Moon Base Mission Progress
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman will host a virtual update on June 30 about the agency's Moon Base mission progress, including new lunar lander mission awards. The discussion will cover plans for building a lunar surface base.

title: Roman Telescope Comes to Kennedy
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center in Florida for launch preparations. The telescope is scheduled to launch no earlier than August 30, 2026.

title: NASA’s HiRISE Captures Perseverance Marking a Milestone on Mars
summary: NASA's Perseverance rover has been captured by the HiRISE camera on Mars, marking a milestone after traveling a full marathon distance of 26.2 miles (42.195 kilometers) on the Red Planet. The rover achieved this feat after five years and four months of driving.

title: Euclid View of Milky Way Heart Previews Core Survey by NASA’s Roman
summary: Euclid, an ESA mission with NASA contributions, has captured a new view of the Milky Way's heart, previewing a survey area that NASA's Roman Space Telescope will observe after its launch this summer. This early data provides astronomers with a head start on Roman's core survey.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4927–4933: Let’s Drive to That Smooth Area
summary: The Curiosity rover's science team is planning a drive to a smoother area based on orbital images showing varied surface textures. The update is provided by a planetary mineralogy professor from The Open University, UK.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. The exhibit aims to educate the public about its legacy after 25 flights.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week's space industry news highlights six orbital launches, including the first Pegasus XL launch, alongside missions by Falcon 9 and Chinese rockets.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle using a Falcon 9 rocket. The test flight took place early Tuesday morning.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is planning repairs for the Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. This marks another busy month of operations aboard the station.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space has transported the second stage of its Terran R heavy-lift rocket to the Stennis Space Center, marking progress toward its planned 2026 debut. The Terran R is being developed as a fully reusable vehicle to support future space missions.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled worldwide in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for the space industry. The launches will take place from various sites across the globe.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket due to prolonged delays. The European private space launch provider is based in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is approaching a static fire test. Flight 12 was successfully completed about three weeks prior.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, marking a significant milestone in its space program. LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry event or development, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet celebrating 50 hallmark achievements over its 50-year history. The publication highlights key milestones and contributions by ESA in the space industry.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the 2025 Council Meeting at Ministerial Level, focusing on industrial opportunities and thematic priorities across ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for versatile applications in the space industry. It highlights the potential of this technology to revolutionize various space operations.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping ESA test and validate materials for spacecraft to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on their innovative ideas to support mission success.

title: Exclusive: SpaceComputer and Spacemanic Partner on Security Demo Mission
summary: SpaceComputer and Spacemanic have partnered to conduct a security demonstration mission. The collaboration aims to address hacking threats and shared-compute modules in rideshare satellites.

title: Japan Eyes Sovereign D2D Satellite Network
summary: Japan is planning to select a proposal for its domestically owned and operated D2D satellite network, called J-LEO. This initiative aims to enhance Japan's sovereign satellite capabilities.

title: Satellite Operators Launch Trade Association
summary: Amazon, Globalstar, Iridium, and Canada’s Telesat have formed the SpaceConnect Association, a new trade group for non-geostationary orbit (NGSO) satellite operators. This collaboration aims to represent and advocate for the interests of NGSO satellite companies.

title: SpaceX Starfall Demo Provides Validation for Reentry Startups
summary: SpaceX launched a demo mission of its Starfall reentry capsule, marking a significant step in the reentry sector. The event highlights growing competition and innovation in space reentry technologies.

title: NASA Watchdog: Launch Infrastructure Nearing Capacity
summary: NASA's launch facilities are expected to reach capacity by the end of the decade due to a surge in commercial launches. A report from the agency's inspector general highlights this growing challenge.

title: Sophia Space Raises $7M, Taps Apex for 2027 Demo Flight
summary: Sophia Space raised $7 million in funding and partnered with Apex for a 2027 demo flight to deploy edge compute hardware in orbit.

title: Ubotica Raises $11M for Satellite-Based AI Tech
summary: Ubotica, a Dublin-based space AI company, raised $11 million to advance its AI-powered intelligence platform for maritime security. The funding will accelerate the commercialization of its satellite-based AI technology.

title: A Faster Path to Orbit: How Hosted Payloads Are Changing Access to Space
summary: Hosted payloads are revolutionizing space access by reducing time, cost, and complexity for deploying new capabilities. Companies no longer need to build entire spacecraft or infrastructure from scratch.

title: Exclusive: Northwood Unveils New Antenna, Expanded Network Capacity
summary: Northwood has unveiled a new antenna to expand its network capacity, supporting more data downlinks over a wider range of frequencies. This development is part of the company's rapid global expansion of its ground station footprint.

title: ElevationSpace Closes $40M Series B
summary: ElevationSpace, a Japanese satellite reentry startup, secured a $40 million Series B funding round, bringing its total raised to $63.5 million.

title: BAE Systems to Build Buses for Vantor’s Vantage Imaging Satellites
summary: BAE Systems will construct the satellite buses for Vantor's Vantage imaging satellites, with the first two expected to be operational by 2029.

title: Space Force Awards Boeing $2 Billion MUOS Service Life Extension Contract
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded Boeing a $2 billion contract to extend the service life of the Mobile User Objective System (MUOS) with two new satellites. The MUOS system, deployed in 2019, provides secure communications for military operations.

title: Amazon Leo to Connect Construction Equipment for Hitachi Construction Machinery
summary: Amazon Leo has partnered with Hitachi Construction Machinery to connect its construction equipment as part of a service preview for enterprises. The collaboration expands Amazon Leo's reach into the construction industry.

title: The EU’s 2 GHz Plan: A Test Case for Sovereignty in Satcom
summary: The European Commission proposed re-authorizing the 2 GHz Mobile Satellite Services band beyond 2027, highlighting satellite connectivity's geopolitical significance. This move is seen as a test case for EU sovereignty in satellite communications.

title: LEO Operators Launch New Industry Group for NGSO Issues
summary: A group of satellite operators including Amazon, Iridium, Telesat, and Globalstar have launched a new trade association called SpaceConnect Association to address Non-Geostationary (NGSO) satellite issues. This initiative aims to represent and advocate for the interests of NGSO satellite operators.

title: Spanish IoT Startup FOSSA Secures $10M in Funding
summary: Spanish IoT startup FOSSA raised $10.5 million to expand its satellite capabilities globally. The funding will support international expansion and sovereign satellite deployment.

title: Northwood Space Debuts Parabolic Antenna, Designed for High-Volume Data
summary: Northwood Space introduced the Prism parabolic antenna, a 2.4-meter ground system designed for high-volume data connectivity. The new technology aims to enhance satellite communications by improving data transfer capabilities.

title: Space Sector Faces 400% Surge in Cyberattacks Amid Iran War, Experts Say
summary: The space sector has experienced a 400% increase in cyberattacks following U.S. and Israel-led military operations in Iran. Cybersecurity experts warn of the rising sophistication and tempo of these attacks.

title: Sophia Space Raises $7M, Selects Apex Space Bus for Orbital Compute Demo
summary: Sophia Space raised $7 million and selected Apex Space's Nova satellite bus for an orbital computing demonstration mission planned for 2027.

title: SpaceX Launches New Microgravity Lab Demo, Starfall
summary: SpaceX demonstrated a new reentry vehicle called Starfall for the first time, aimed at enabling regular access to microgravity for science and in-space manufacturing.
